data.title: Alcoa Appoints Thomas Gorman as Non-Executive Chairman
data.description:
Alcoa said late Thursday it has named Thomas Gorman as non-executive chairman of the board.

Gorman, a board member since May 2021, succeeded Steven Williams, who stepped down after more than eight years of service.

data.title: India's Nifty, Sensex set to snap winning streak as India-Pakistan hostilities rise
data.description:
** India's Nify 50 and Sensex set to snap its longest weekly winning streak of 2025 on heightened volatility as India-Pakistan conflicts intensify

** The indexes down around 1.5% for the week, after rising nearly 7% in the previous three weeks

** India and Pakistan accused each other of launching new military attacks on Friday, using drones and artillery for the third day in the worst fighting between the nuclear-armed South Asian neighbours in nearly three decades

** Sun Pharmaceutical Industries and Jio Financial Services fall nearly 5% each, set to be biggest pct losers among Nifty 50 stocks for the week

** Tata Motors and Titan Company jump 7.7% and 4.8%, on course for biggest weekly Nifty 50 pct gainers

data.title: Indian generic drugmakers set for weekly drop on potential US pharma policy changes
data.description:
** Shares of Indian generic drugmakers down between 2% to 5% this week

** Sun Pharmaceuticals down 4.8% this week, set for sharpest weekly drop since June 2022

** U.S. President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Monday that aims to reduce the time it takes to approve pharmaceutical plants in the country

** Cipla and Dr Reddy's Laboratories down 3.49% and ~2% this week, respectively

** The three drugmakers, having a combined 46% weightage on the pharma index , get between 30% to 40% of revenue from North America

** Pharma index is down 2.7% this week

data.title: Bulgaria's retail sales rise 5.4% y/y in March
data.description:
SOFIA (Bulgaria), May 9 (SeeNews) - Bulgaria's retail sales at constant prices, except for motor vehicles and motorcycles, went up 5.4% year-on-year in March on the back of higher sales across all observed groups, the country's statistical office said on Friday, citing preliminary data.

In February, Bulgaria's retail sales rose by 2.4% on the year, the National Statistical Institute (NSI) said in a monthly report.

Retail sales of automotive fuel in specialised stores grew by an annual 8.3% in March, while sales of non-food products added 7.2% and sales of food, beverages and tobacco climbed by 0.5%.

On a seasonally-adjusted monthly comparison basis, retail trade turnover at constant prices advanced by 1.1% in March against a 1.6% decline the previous month.

Details follow (pct change):

March y/yMarch m/mFeb y/y*Feb m/m*
Retail sales5.41.12.4-1.6
-food, drinks, tobacco0.50.9-2.1-1.7
-non-food (excl fuel)7.20.93.7-1.4
-fuel8.3-1.911.5-2.7

-food, drinks, tobacco

*revised data

source: NSI

data.title: Leonardo Appears Close to Turning the Page on Aerostructures' Weakness — Market Talk
data.description: Leonardo appears close to turning the page on its weakest division, Aerostructures, Alpha Value analyst Saima Hussain says. The Italian aerospace and defense group is working on a deal, most likely a joint venture with a large international partner, possibly Saudi, she says. Leonardo hasn't named the partner but has signed an exclusive memorandum of understanding and due diligence is underway, including site visits to the four key plants, Hussain notes. The goal is to finalize the partnership framework by July and sign the deal by year-end. "This would mark the definitive fix to Aerostructures' chronic losses, and the clearest structural catalyst for re-rating the division in years," Hussain writes in a note to investors. Shares are down 2% at 48.02 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

data.title: China launches campaign to crack down on critical mineral smuggling
data.description:
China has launched a special campaign to crack down on the smuggling of strategic minerals as Beijing seeks to enforce export restrictions it placed on various metals used in industries ranging from defence to clean energy.

Since China imposed controls on metals such as gallium, germanium, antimony, tungsten and some rare earths, some overseas entities have "colluded with domestic lawbreakers" to evade the export restrictions, according to a statement from the ministry of commerce on Friday.

The campaign, which was announced at a conference in the southern city of Shenzhen, urges government departments to target typical evasion methods such as false reporting, concealment, smuggling, and trans-shipment through third countries, the statement said.

Across the border in Hong Kong, authorities in March seized a cargo of antimony ingots under an ordinance that can apply to the export of controlled items without a licence. No explanation was given for the seizure.

About 3.9 million kg of wrought and unwrought antimony were exported from China last year, however shipments have all but stopped since September when the export controls were implemented.

As of early April, the only export was a 20,000-kg cargo to Japan in January, according to Chinese customs data.

data.title: Market Chatter: Singapore, China to Boost Trade Cooperation to Help Stave Off Tariff Impact
data.description:
Singapore will boost its international trade cooperation with China to boost businesses among the two Asian nations, Yicai Global reported Thursday, citing former Singapore Chinese Chamber of Commerce & Industry President Roland Ng San Tiong.

The chamber will help companies globalize and bring in investments amid the trade war with China and the US, the report said, citing Ng.

China and Singapore hosted an annual Conference on International Industrial Cooperation in Shanghai on May 6.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Qinchuan Machine Tool to Receive 80 Million Yuan for Land Requisition
data.description:
Qinchuan Machine Tool & Tool Group said the Xi'an Economic Development Management Committee will acquire its land use rights and buildings in Weiyang District, Xi'an City in China.

The estimated compensation totals 80.0 million yuan, including 49.5 million yuan for the land and buildings and 30.5 million yuan in land transfer reward, according to a Friday filing with the Shenzhen bourse.

The deal is subject to shareholder approval.

Shares of the machine tools manufacturer closed 2% lower Friday.
